description_long: This is a military-style helicopter with a streamlined, dark green fuselage and a long tail boom. The helicopter features a large main rotor with multiple blades and a smaller tail rotor at the end of the tail boom. The cockpit is located at the front, with several angular, transparent windows providing visibility for the pilots. The landing gear consists of two robust wheels and a rear support, designed for stability during takeoff and landing. Along the sides, there are external attachments that may serve as weapon mounts or equipment racks, typical of utility or combat helicopters. The surface finish is mostly matte with some subtle gloss, and the overall build suggests a metal-dominant construction with reinforced structural elements.
description: A dark green military-style helicopter with a large main rotor, angular cockpit windows, and robust landing gear. The fuselage is streamlined with external attachments and a long tail boom.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, main rotor, tail boom, and landing gear.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the main rotor blade arrangement and the narrow profile of the fuselage.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail rotor, main rotor, and external attachments.
description_view_3: Frontal view focusing on the cockpit windows, nose, and symmetrical landing gear.
attribute_rotor_blades: The main rotor has five long, straight blades made of composite or metal, colored black, with no visible markings. The blades are evenly spaced and extend well beyond the width of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_rotor: The tail rotor is present at the end of the tail boom, featuring three short, black blades arranged perpendicularly to the tail. No protective shroud or markings are visible.
attribute_landing_skids: This helicopter uses a wheeled landing gear system rather than skids, with two large black wheels on either side and a smaller rear support wheel.
attribute_cockpit_windows: The cockpit has five angular, transparent windows: two large front-facing, two side, and one smaller lower window. The windows are framed in black and appear slightly tinted.
